mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a contraction in the industrial production index reflects persistent supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during post-shock recovery periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in stronger fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, accelerating inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 220, invalidating the inflation-tightening transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement of PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 3-year forward testing period.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, decelerating retail sales growth relative to prior quarters indicates consumer demand saturation, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration during late-cycle expansions is followed by positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.
